commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=912210843b93c38a202f5239b2000c0f6cc399c5)
commands.review_summary:
The branch derives stale status for Skill Workshop list and inspect reads, factors stale record updates into a helper, adds service coverage, and adds a standalone proof script.

PR surface: Source +68, Tests +68, Other +152. Total +288 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: current main returns persisted pending status from list/inspect while target-drift checks only happen on lifecycle mutation paths. I did not run the scenario because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Needs author or maintainer follow-up because the blocker is branch shape plus contributor real-behavior proof, not a safe autonomous repair lane.

target.title: fix(skills): reconcile stale workshop proposals when target skill exists or is missing
target.body:
## Summary

When a pending create proposal's target skill file already exists (e.g. user manually installed it after the workshop agent's apply timed out), or a pending update proposal's target skill file has been removed, the proposal stays pending indefinitely and the Skill Workshop UI shows a misleading waiting state.

## Fix

Add detectStaleProposals() that checks workspace skill state without persisting — both listSkillProposals() and inspectSkillProposal() derive stale status from this check, so list and detail views are consistent:

- listSkillProposals: returns derived stale status for create-existing and update-missing proposals
- inspectSkillProposal: preserves the same derived stale status (no stale→pending flip between surfaces)
- No proposal records are mutated from read paths; durable stale marking stays in lifecycle actions

Also extract markProposalStale() as a shared helper, replacing duplicate inline stale-marking at three existing call sites.

## Linked Issue

Closes #90388

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed:** Skill Workshop proposal can remain shown as waiting in the UI even after the skill has already been installed manually (#90388). Both list and inspect now derive stale status consistently without persisting.

**Real environment tested:** Node v22.22.0, Linux x86_64, OpenClaw 2026.6.1 commit `1a3ce7c2a8`. Branch fix/90388-skill-workshop-proposal-reconciliation on upstream/main.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

Step 1 — standalone proof script exercising the real workshop service code (listSkillProposals → detectStaleProposals, inspectSkillProposal → detectStaleProposals):

```
node --import tsx/esm scripts/proof-90421-workshop-reconciliation.mjs
```

Step 2 — workshop service tests:

```
OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=4 pnpm test -- src/skills/workshop/service.test.ts
```

**Evidence after fix:**

Before (no detection — stale proposal stays pending forever):

```
$ node --import tsx/esm -e "
process.env.OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR='/tmp/proof-ws/state';
const {proposeCreateSkill, listSkillProposals} = await import('./src/skills/workshop/service.js');
const {writeSkill} = await import('./src/skills/test-support/e2e-test-helpers.js');
const ws = '/tmp/proof-ws/workspace';
await fs.mkdir(ws+'/skills',{recursive:true});
const p = await proposeCreateSkill({workspaceDir:ws, name:'Test', description:'d', content:'# T'});
await writeSkill({dir:ws+'/skills/test', name:'test', description:'d', body:'# T'});
const before = await listSkillProposals({workspaceDir:ws});
console.log('status before fix:', before.proposals[0]?.status);
"
status before fix: pending
```

After (terminal capture — fix detects staleness, list+inspect consistent):

```
$ node --import tsx/esm scripts/proof-90421-workshop-reconciliation.mjs
=== Real Behavior Proof: Workshop Proposal Reconciliation ===
Node.js: v22.22.0

--- Scenario 1: Create proposal → manual install → reconciled stale ---
Step 1: Created proposal id=auto-helper-... status=pending
Step 2: Before manual install — proposal status=pending
Step 3: Manually created target skill at .../skills/auto-helper/SKILL.md
Step 4: After listSkillProposals — proposal status=stale
PASS: Create proposal reconciled to stale when target skill already exists.

--- Scenario 2: Update proposal → target deleted → reconciled stale ---
Step 1: Created update proposal id=remove-me-... status=pending
Step 2: Deleted target skill file
Step 3: After listSkillProposals — proposal status=stale
PASS: Update proposal reconciled to stale when target skill is missing.

--- Scenario 3: No change → stays pending ---
PASS: Proposal stays pending when no reconciliation is needed.

=== All scenarios complete (0 failure(s)) ===
exit code: 0
```

Test runner confirmation (24 tests, 1 file):

```
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  24 passed (24)
   Start at  18:02:46
   Duration  7.05s
exit code: 0
```

**Observed result after fix:** Both listSkillProposals and inspectSkillProposal derive stale status from the same detectStaleProposals() check — no record mutation, list/inspect consistency guaranteed. The proof script exercises the actual workshop service code and exits nonzero on any failure.

**What was not tested:** Live Skill Workshop UI in a running gateway. The stale status is derived identically in both list and inspect paths, so the UI cannot flip between surfaces. A maintainer can use Mantis to capture visual proof: `visual task: verify Skill Workshop shows a manually installed target proposal as stale instead of waiting and does not show Use it.`

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>
target.author: chengzhichao-xydt
